tags.name: Blue Belt
tags.note: Toyota wreck (103m, 2399 GRT, built 1950 Howaldtswerke Hamburg), struck Shaab Suedi reef 2 Dec 1977. Depth reported inconsistently 15-85m to 21-90m across sources; cassiopeiasafari.com reports wreck may no longer be open for diving and has continued sliding deeper - confirm access with operator. Only 2 independent domains verified; needs 3rd source before marking fully validated.

tags.name: Gota Mesharifa
tags.note: UNRESOLVED naming/feature conflict: sources describe a 'Mesharifa' sand-island/lagoon manta ray cleaning station (Aug-Nov per dive-the-world.com, Oct-Jun per theDiveGlobe - season windows themselves conflict), but this record is a submerged pinnacle/wall per OSM tagging. No source confirms these are the same location. Manta and coral/shark claims from earlier draft removed as unverifiable for this specific GPS point. Difficulty floored to Advanced per destination policy.

tags.name: Sweet Potato
tags.note: Grouper aggregation site, 30+ potato groupers, strong currents

tags.description: Experimantal unterwater station Precontinent II run by Jacques-Yves Cousteau in 1963
tags.name: Shaab Rumi, Precontinent 2
tags.note: Conshelf II (1963): ScubaBoard diver thread confirms the main 'Starfish House' living quarters were salvaged/removed after the experiment - only the submarine hangar, tool shed, and shark/fish cages remain on the seabed, heavily encrusted. Depth of remains ~9-20m for hangar/cages, ~27-30m for Deep Cabin site (sources vary on exact figure). Distance from Port Sudan reported inconsistently 30-48km across sources. Grey reef, silky, and silvertip sharks confirmed at Sha'ab Rumi generally by 2 sources; hammerheads Nov-Apr. No site-specific marine life confirmed for the habitat structures themselves. Difficulty floored to Advanced per destination policy (general dive literature would rate the lagoon Intermediate).

tags.name: Daharat Abida
tags.note: Likely = "Dahrat Abid" in dive-the-world.com; southernmost named Sudan site, ~220km S of Port Sudan, ~30km from Eritrean border. Hammerhead schools (count varies 50-100 across sources, treated as anecdotal). Wreck on N/NW side confirmed by 2 sources but "3 wrecks" claim NOT independently corroborated - removed from markdown pending further verification.

tags.name: Merlo Reef
tags.note: Location corrected: ~1.7nm (~2-3km) SW of Angarosh, not '~70km north of Port Sudan' as previously stated. Holds wreckage of liveaboard 'Freedom' which ran aground 1999 (confirmed by dive-the-world.com + divescover.com; zentacle.com/wannadive.net independently confirm an unnamed wreck on NW side, consistent but not independently naming it). Depth range varies by source: 20-21m avg, max reported 38-50m depending on source (wannadive) vs ~21m max (zentacle) - likely reflects crest vs wall. Current reported as generally mild (<1 knot per wannadive), milder than previously described. Marine life confirmed for this site specifically: giant moray eels, anemonefish, goatfish, snappers, unicornfish, whitetip reef sharks, Napoleon wrasse, butterflyfish (dive-the-world.com); sharks and occasional mantas (zentacle/wannadive). Difficulty floored to Advanced per destination policy (wannadive rates it Intermediate/CMAS**-AOW in general terms).

tags.name: Shaab Jimna
tags.note: Likely = "Shaab Jibna"/"Shaab Jumna" in dive-the-world.com and other sources, ~27nm/~50km S/SE of Port Sudan. Cave/crevice topography CONFIRMED by multiple independent operators (not fabricated). Hammerhead schools confirmed (one report: ~50 sharks at ~30m, treated as example not guarantee).

tags.name: Dibsel Island
tags.note: UNRESOLVED: No site-specific source found under this name or plausible spelling variants (Dissul, Dibsil, Dubsel, etc.) despite extensive search of dive-the-world.com, Blue Force Fleet, Cassiopeia Safari, Royal Evolution, Zentacle. Coordinates place it in the same deep-south cluster as Karam Marsmarit/Daharat Abida (~165km S of Port Sudan) so plausibly a real, locally-named reef, but unconfirmed. Do not mark validated until a site-specific source is found. Marine life/dive profile content in markdown rewritten to explicitly state lack of site-specific sourcing.

tags.name: Isar Island
tags.note: UNRESOLVED: No site-specific source found under this name or plausible spelling variants despite extensive search of dive-the-world.com, Blue Force Fleet (both extreme-south and central-south pages), Cassiopeia Safari, Royal Evolution, PADI Suakin listings. Considered possible match to "Seil Ada Kebir" (Turtle Island) but NOT confirmed as same site - flagging for orchestrator review, not merging. Coordinates place it in deep-south cluster near Daharat Abida/Karam Marsmarit. Marine life/dive profile content rewritten to explicitly state lack of site-specific sourcing.

tags.name: Shab Rumi, Plateau S
tags.note: Only 2 independent domains found (below the 3-domain validation threshold); both confirm Sha'ab Rumi generally (grey reef, silky, silvertip sharks; hammerheads Nov-Apr; barracuda schools) but neither source specifically distinguishes a 'South Plateau' sector from the rest of Sha'ab Rumi. Earlier draft's 'barracuda tornado' and plateau-specific superlatives could not be traced to any source and were removed. Difficulty floored to Advanced per destination policy.

tags.name: Elba Reef
tags.note: Two documented plateaus (North, South), 20-45m. Geography flagged: reef straddles Egypt-Sudan border but is dived almost exclusively as part of Egypt Deep South liveaboard itineraries (Port Ghalib departure), not Sudan/Port Sudan itineraries. Country classification conflicts between sources (Royal Evolution calls it Sudanese; Sadko/Divebooker call it Egyptian).

tags.name: Karam Marsmarit
tags.note: Confirmed = "Karam Masamirit" (alt. spelling), ~2nm SE of Masamirit Island, Suakin Islands. SE wall/drop-off with sharks (grey reef, silvertip, hammerhead, occasional tiger per one source) and napoleon wrasse confirmed by 3 independent domains. Silky shark/oceanic whitetip/manta claims removed - not site-specific in sources reviewed.

tags.name: Abu Seif
tags.note: Shallow relaxed reef, cracks and crevices, excellent visibility, clownfish and anemones

tags.name: Sanganeb Plauteua N
tags.note: CORRECTED: lighthouse built 1906 by the British (Wikipedia List of lighthouses in Sudan + wtreportage.net both agree), not 1870 as previously stated - 1870 figure unverifiable and removed. Original steel tower replaced with current conical stone tower in 1955 per wtreportage.net (not independently corroborated, kept as single-source note). Sanganeb + Dungonab Bay-Mukkawar Island jointly UNESCO-inscribed in 2016 as 'Sanganeb Marine National Park and Dungonab Bay - Mukkawar Island Marine National Park' (confirmed via UNESCO WHC listing). Hammerhead, grey reef, and whitetip reef sharks plus Napoleon wrasse and humphead parrotfish confirmed for Sanganeb generally by 2 sources, but not specific to this North Plateau GPS point. Manta/eagle ray claims from earlier draft removed as unverifiable for this location. Difficulty floored to Advanced per destination policy.

tags.name: Elba Reef, South Plateau
tags.note: South Plateau (with SS Isola di Levanzo wreck at its edge) corroborated by 4 independent domains: large pinnacles, ~20-45m depth. NOTE: this conflicts with this record depth (14m) and prior Intermediate difficulty -- sources describe South Plateau as a deep pinnacle structure, not a shallow 5-14m shelf. Difficulty raised to Advanced per destination minimum; depth field flagged for review, not changed by this audit.

tags.name: Elba Reef, North Plateau
tags.note: North Plateau corroborated by 3 independent domains: ~20-45m, coral fan formations along drop-off. Site-specific marine life beyond grey reef sharks/hammerheads (general Elba Reef fauna) not corroborated; removed padding (pygmy seahorses, resident Napoleon wrasse, groupers, lionfish).

tags.name: SS Umbria, Wreck
tags.note: Ex-Bahia Blanca (built Hamburg, launched 30 Dec 1911), renamed Umbria 1935, scuttled by own crew at Wingate Reef 9-10 June 1940 to deny cargo to British. Length/tonnage reported as 154.9m/10,076 GRT (Wikipedia) vs ~150m (PADI, no tonnage given). Depth reported inconsistently: 38m max (Wikipedia, cassiopeiasafari) vs 5-35m (PADI). Difficulty raised from Intermediate to Advanced per destination minimum policy for remote Sudan liveaboard sites.

tags.name: Elba Reef, West-Plateau
tags.note: No independent source found documenting a distinct West Plateau as a separately named Elba Reef dive site. Only North and South plateaus are documented. May originate from Royal Evolution mention of drifting along the west side of the North Plateau (Anemon City feature) -- a directional descriptor, not a separate charted site. Marine life/profile specifics stripped as unverifiable. Difficulty already Advanced (unchanged).

tags.name: Levanzo Wreck
tags.note: Ex-Isola di Levanzo, built 1901 by Fratelli Orlando (Livorno), renamed Levanzo 1902; grounded on Elba Reef 14 Mar 1923, sank during failed salvage 28 Mar 1923. Length corrected: previous 113m figure unconfirmed, sources give 339.6ft (~103.5m)/3713 GRT. Depth reported inconsistently: stern 20-22m consistent, but deeper sections reported 40-45m by some sources vs 65-75m by others - unresolved conflict. Only 2 independent domains directly verified; needs 3rd source before marking fully validated.

tags.name: Elba Reef East (Southplateau)
tags.note: No independent source found documenting a distinct East (Southplateau) as a separately named Elba Reef dive site. Only North and South plateaus are documented; Levanzo wreck sits at South Plateau edge, described by one ScubaBoard poster as eastern region of Elba reef which may be the origin of this label. Marine life/profile specifics stripped as unverifiable. Difficulty raised from Intermediate to Advanced per destination minimum policy.
